summary: Matched Ganondorf fighter code by replacing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftganon.s with src/melee/ft/chara/ftganon.c and a new ftganon.h, moving ftganon.o from asm to src in obj_files.mk and adding it to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED. The C implementation covers Ganondorf on-death/on-load hooks and several item/part-animation helpers. Review identified that lbl_804D9E58 was not a symbol that needed an extern but a 0.0f float constant; using literal 0.0f plus deleting the old .s produced the match. A remaining mismatch was traced to sdata2 alignment in the following ftemblem asm file, fixed by adding .balign 8 before lbl_804D9E60.

key_files.role: Deleted matched assembly implementation for Ganondorf fighter code.
New C implementation for ftGanon_OnDeath, ftGanon_OnLoad, and related func_8014EC58 through func_8014EEAC helpers.
New header declaring the Ganondorf functions and including HSD_GObj and Fighter types.
Added prototypes for ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, func_800E2AAC, and func_800E2B40 used by ftganon.c.
Switched ftganon.o from the asm object path to the src object path.
Added .balign 8 in .sdata2 before lbl_804D9E60 to repair alignment after removing ftganon.s sdata2 data.

what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftganon.c with C versions of ftGanon_OnDeath, func_8014EC58, func_8014ED38, func_8014ED80, func_8014EDC8, ftGanon_OnLoad, func_8014EE40, func_8014EE60, and func_8014EEAC.
Implemented ftGanon_OnDeath to call func_80074A4C for parts 0 and 1, then clear Fighter fields x2230 and x222C.
Implemented held-item checks using func_8026B2B4 and func_8026B320 on ft->x1974_heldItem, dispatching switch cases 1 through 4 to func_80070FB4 with mapped values 1, 0, 2, and 3.
Implemented animation helper functions func_8014EE60 and func_8014EEAC with ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim calls on parts 3 and 4, using literal 0.0f for the float argument.
Added ftganon.h declarations for the new C file.
Updated include/functions.h to give typed prototypes for ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim(struct _HSD_GObj*, s32, s32, f32), func_800E2AAC(struct _Fighter*), and func_800E2B40(struct _HSD_GObj*).
Changed obj_files.mk so ftganon.o is built from src/melee/ft/chara/ftganon.o instead of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftganon.o.
Added .balign 8 to ftemblem.s .sdata2 before lbl_804D9E60 to fix downstream sdata2 alignment.
smart_moves: Converted the old lbl_804D9E58 sdata2 zero value into direct 0.0f literals in ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim calls, avoiding an unnecessary extern and matching the intended float constant usage.
After the C code still failed to match, the author checked the asmdiff and recognized the mismatch was not necessarily in ftganon text logic but in data-section placement/alignment.
Fixed the following file's .sdata2 alignment with .balign 8 in ftemblem.s, preserving section layout after deleting ftganon.s and its sdata2 contribution.
Kept unclassified function names as address-style func_8014xxxx where no stronger names were evidenced, while using known hook names ftGanon_OnDeath and ftGanon_OnLoad.
decomp_lessons: When replacing an asm file that owns .sdata2 constants, check neighboring object files for section-alignment fallout; deleting data can shift later constants even if the new C text logic is correct.
A small-data symbol containing zero may just represent a float literal. If review or disassembly indicates it is a constant, prefer a typed literal such as 0.0f over carrying an extern label into C.
A full asm-to-C match requires build-list updates as well as source code: move the object from asm to src in obj_files.mk and update epilogue lists when applicable.
Asmdiff differences around apparent instruction/data words near section boundaries can indicate alignment or sdata2 layout problems rather than incorrect C control flow.
Adding precise prototypes in include/functions.h can be necessary for matching calls that pass HSD_GObj*, Fighter*, s32, and f32 arguments.
naming_conventions: Character-specific hook names use the pattern ftGanon_OnDeath and ftGanon_OnLoad.
Unidentified local functions retained address-derived names such as func_8014EC58, func_8014ED38, func_8014ED80, func_8014EDC8, func_8014EE40, func_8014EE60, and func_8014EEAC.
Header guard used the project-local lowercase style _ftganon_h_.
Fighter state fields in use include x1974_heldItem, x222C, and x2230; no semantic field names were introduced in this PR.
Known cross-module function prototype ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim was promoted from an unknown comment to a typed declaration.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Delete the matched .s file once the C version and object-list switch produce the same output.
Replace lbl_804D9E58 with 0.0f when the label is only a float zero constant used as an f32 argument.
Use asmdiff to localize residual mismatches; in this PR the cited diff showed shifted words around addresses 43383c-43384c, leading to a section-alignment fix.
Add .balign 8 before ftemblem.s lbl_804D9E60 in .sdata2 to maintain expected alignment after ftganon.s removal.
For switch reconstruction, mapping held-item result cases 1, 2, 3, and 4 to separate func_80070FB4 calls reproduced the original branch structure.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Delete the .s file and replace lbl_804D9E58 with 0.0F to get a full match because it is a float constant.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Reported an asmdiff mismatch around shifted data/instruction-looking words after attempting the suggested fix.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Confirmed the match after fixing alignment of the following file's sdata2 section.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where deleting an asm file required adding .balign in the following file's .sdata2 section.
Search for remaining extern lbl_804Dxxxx float-zero constants that could be replaced with 0.0f literals.
Inspect later renames or documentation for func_8014EC58 through func_8014EEAC to recover semantic Ganondorf helper names.
Check whether ftganon.h header guard style was later normalized to project conventions.
